In works such as The Persistence of Memory, he uses meticulous realism to render impossible objects—melting watches, distorted bodies, barren dream landscapes—so that the unconscious appears as a vivid hallucination.     "magritte_approach": "Magritte’s approach is cooler and more philosophical.
